---
title: "How it works"
description: "The ideas that make everything else make sense — read once, apply everywhere."
order: 0
---

The ideas below explain how OpenPipeline is put together and why it behaves the
way it does. Read them top to bottom for the full picture, or jump to whichever
answers your question.

```{=html}
<div class="concept-list">

  <a class="concept-card" href="ecosystem.html">
    <div class="ct">The OpenPipeline ecosystem</div>
    <p>One core package, four satellites — all built the same way, sharing the same components and data format.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="modularity.html">
    <div class="ct">True modularity</div>
    <p>Modular at the execution level, not just in the source tree: every step is an independently built, containerized unit.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <div class="concept-card">
    <div class="ct">Components &amp; workflows</div>
    <p>A <b>module</b> does one job (normalize, cluster, filter). A <b>workflow</b> chains modules into a pipeline. Both are built with <a href="viash.html"><b>Viash</b></a> and containerized, so a local run and a cloud run behave identically.</p>
  </div>

  <a class="concept-card" href="mudata.html">
    <div class="ct">MuData as standard data format</div>
    <p>One HDF5 object every component reads and writes — with converters to and from H5AD, Seurat, TileDB at the edges.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="viash.html">
    <div class="ct">The engine: Viash and Viash Hub</div>
    <p>How components are built, containerized, tested, and published — and why the reference can be auto-generated.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="param-list.html">
    <div class="ct">Many samples at once: the param_list</div>
    <p>Process a whole cohort in one run, with per-sample arguments inside the list and shared settings outside it.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card" href="architecture.html">
    <div class="ct">Pipeline architecture</div>
    <p>How a dataset flows from raw sequencing to an annotated object, including QC reporting and the spatial branch.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

  <a class="concept-card blue" href="data-state.html">
    <div class="ct">Advanced: the data-state view</div>
    <p>Under the hood, processing is organized by the <em>state of the data</em> (single-sample → multi-sample → merged → integrated). A deep-dive for pipeline authors.</p>
    <span class="go">Read →</span>
  </a>

</div>
```
